Causes the authenticated user to follow a specific user by their ID or username. Query params: id (string, optional) - numeric user ID to follow; username (string, optional) - screen name to follow (one of id/username required); auth_token (string, required) - Twitter auth_token cookie; ct0 (string, required) - Twitter ct0 cookie. Returns { following: true } on success.

Adds a Post to the authenticated user's Bookmarks by its ID. Query params: id (string, required) - numeric tweet ID to bookmark; auth_token (string, required) - Twitter auth_token cookie; ct0 (string, required) - Twitter ct0 cookie. Returns { bookmarked: true } on success.

Causes the authenticated user to Like a specific Post by its ID. Query params: id (string, required) - numeric tweet ID to like; auth_token (string, required) - Twitter auth_token cookie; ct0 (string, required) - Twitter ct0 cookie. Returns { liked: true } on success.

Causes the authenticated user to repost a specific Post by its ID. Query params: id (string, required) - numeric tweet ID to retweet; auth_token (string, required) - Twitter auth_token cookie; ct0 (string, required) - Twitter ct0 cookie. Returns { retweeted: true } on success.

options.text: You're absolutely right to be frustrated...

script.example_phrase: I can see this is the second time we've gotten your order wrong, and I completely understand your frustration.

resources.description: Personality-driven persuasion strategy — multi-step influence plan using Cialdini principles, ELM routing, cognitive bias applications, and personality-specific levers. Outcome-oriented: moves a person toward a specific decision.

body.objective: Get the prospect to sign the annual enterprise contract

strategies.example_phrase: Teams like yours at Acme saw 40% faster onboarding.
Every month on the old plan costs roughly $12K in manual overhead.

cognitive_biases: Anchoring — lead with the premium tier
Loss aversion — frame as cost of NOT switching
example.persuasion_arc: Build credibility through social proof, anchor on ROI, close with commitment consistency

resources.description: Real-time linguistic style matching — analyzes recent messages and returns precise communication parameters (tone, formality, energy, directness, humor, pacing) plus an injectable mirror prompt. Designed for frequent mid-conversation calls.

messages: Hey, just wanted to check in on the timeline. No rush but would be good to have a rough idea.
Thanks for the update! That makes sense. Let me loop in Sarah and we can figure out next steps together.

example.mirror_prompt: Use a warm, casual tone. Keep sentences medium-length. Be collaborative — use 'we' and 'together'. Light on formality.

resources.description: Subtext analysis — reads between the lines to detect hidden objections, unspoken concerns, power moves, testing behavior, buying signals, and emotional undercurrent. Input intelligence that makes every other BLAH endpoint more effective.

body.message: We appreciate the proposal and will definitely circle back after reviewing internally. There are just a few things we'd like to align on first.

example.literal_meaning: They acknowledge the proposal and plan to review it internally before following up

subtext.reading: They're stalling — 'circle back' and 'align internally' are delay signals
subtext.evidence: 'circle back' and 'reviewing internally' are classic deflection phrases

subtext.reading: They have concerns but aren't ready to voice them directly
subtext.evidence: 'a few things we'd like to align on' suggests unspoken objections

objections: Price or scope concerns not yet surfaced
concerns: Internal alignment challenges — may need buy-in from others
power_moves: 'Reviewing internally' creates distance and shifts control

example.recommended_interpretation: They're interested but face internal hurdles. The delay is about politics, not rejection.
response_considerations: Offer to help with internal alignment

resources.description: Multi-turn negotiation intelligence — ZOPA/BATNA analysis, concession sequencing, anchor calibration, bluff detection, and power dynamics assessment. Personality-aware strategy when KYH data is provided.

strategy.opening_move: Reframe from price to total value of ownership

bluff_detection: Watch for 'other vendors' without naming specifics
Budget limits that shift during conversation

power_dynamics.assessment: Agent holds moderate advantage — unique offering with limited alternatives

resources.description: Information framing — optimal presentation of difficult news, trade-offs, price increases, feedback, and controversial decisions. Personality-calibrated when KYH data is provided. Framing science: gain/loss, temporal, attribution, and scope framing.

body.information: We need to increase your plan price by 25% starting next month due to infrastructure costs
body.audience_context: Long-term enterprise customer, 3 years, high usage
body.desired_outcome: Customer accepts the increase and sees it as fair

recommended_frame.opening: I wanted to reach out personally about an update to your plan.
recommended_frame.body: Over the past year, we've invested heavily in the capabilities your team relies on most. To sustain that and deliver what's next, we're adjusting pricing to $X/month starting next quarter.
recommended_frame.close: You've been with us since the beginning — I'd love to walk through how to maximize value under the new structure.

example.framing_strategy: Value-first gain framing with relationship anchoring

alternative_frames.strategy: Compare to higher market rates first

resources.description: Real-time relationship health scoring from conversation history. Trust trajectory, warmth signals, rapport breaks, engagement trend, and repair suggestions. Cheap enough to call every few turns.

history.text: Hi! I saw you checked out our enterprise plan. Happy to answer any questions.
Yeah, we're evaluating a few options. What's your uptime guarantee?
We guarantee 99.9% uptime with SLA-backed credits. We've exceeded that every quarter this year.
For 50 seats, you're looking at $15K/year. Want me to send a formal quote?

repair_suggestions: Add a personal touch — ask about their current setup
Show curiosity about their evaluation criteria
example.trajectory_summary: Transactional but not cold. The human is in evaluation mode — engaged but guarded. No rapport breaks, but also no warmth building. Opportunity to deepen before the formal quote.

resources.description: Win/Loss Deal Analysis — post-deal retrospective analyzing what worked, what failed, and lessons for this buyer personality type. Returns turning points, personality fit score, and reengagement strategy.

what_worked: Strong initial demo resonated with VP Sales
what_failed: Didn't address CTO security concerns proactively
missed_opportunities: Could have provided SOC 2 report before CTO review

personality_fit.assessment: Approach was optimized for the VP (driver type) but missed the CTO's analytical decision style

turning_points.lesson: Address technical stakeholders' concerns before they become deal blockers

recommended_adjustments: Lead with security documentation for analytical buyers
Map the full committee before demo stage
example.reengagement_strategy: Wait 6 weeks, re-approach with completed SOC 2 report and security architecture walkthrough

Agent-to-human endpoint to ask for human opinions, assistance, or feedback: create a paid task on wurk.fun and fetch human replies. Write clear, structured instructions (you can include URLs to images/video/audio for context). Best for questions an average internet user can answer; avoid highly niche or expert-only prompts. Examples: quick opinions/polls, product or UI feedback, choosing between options, reviewing a piece of content, rewriting short copy variants, summarizing a page, basic tagging/categorization, and general “what do you think?” help. Use action=view with your secret (or the returned statusUrl) to fetch submissions, and action=recover to list your recent tasks.
Recover recent agent-to-human jobs (action=recover). Minimal fee ~0.001 USDC.

Solana Pump.fun Comments quick endpoint. Create a paid WURK task to get comments on a Pump.fun token page. Provide a pump.fun URL (and optional amount + optional instructions), pay with USDC via x402, and the job will be created on wurk.fun for users to comment and submit proof.

Agent-to-human endpoint to ask for human opinions, assistance, or feedback: create a paid task on wurk.fun and fetch human replies. Write clear, structured instructions (you can include URLs to images/video/audio for context). Best for questions an average internet user can answer; avoid highly niche or expert-only prompts. Examples: quick opinions/polls, product or UI feedback, choosing between options, reviewing a piece of content, rewriting short copy variants, summarizing a page, basic tagging/categorization, and general “what do you think?” help. Use action=view with your secret (or the returned statusUrl) to fetch submissions, and action=recover to list your recent tasks.
Recover recent agent-to-human jobs (action=recover). Minimal fee ~0.001 USDC.
